name: Texas Muslim Womens Foundation Inc
mission: Founded in 2005, Texas Muslim Women's Foundation (TMWF) aims to empower, promote and support all women and their families, with a special focus on North Texas. We are an equal opportunity provider. We engage all members of the community – women, men, seniors, families and youth through our various programs. We provide social and family violence services that foster peace and well-being in the home through our Peace in the Home Services and Peaceful Oasis Shelter. In addition, TMWF also arranges leadership skills development for Muslim youth to inspire the next generation to build a promising future for our society and educational programs for Muslim and non-Muslim community members to learn about topics essential to individual and community welfare.
theoryOfChange: The report 'From the Roots of Trauma to the Flowering of Trauma-Informed Care,' published in 2020, details the Texas Muslim Women's Foundation's (TMWF) culturally-sensitive trauma-informed model. It outlines their process of becoming a trauma-informed agency, including learning about types of trauma and trauma-informed care, assessing existing practices, identifying new ones, training staff, and working with researchers to document and build evidence-based practice. The report also mentions conducting needs assessments, process evaluations, and outcome evaluations to assess implementation fidelity and identify intended changes.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high alignment for Muslim donors due to its zakat-eligible status and focus on domestic poverty, a high-urgency cause [2][8]. With a total revenue of $3,769,366 and a program expense ratio of 86.0%, the organization maintains a strong focus on its core mission of providing social services and youth leadership development [1][3].

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization provides detailed annual counts of survivors served and housed, though it lacks independent RCT evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: TMWF uses a holistic approach combining immediate safety (shelter) with long-term stability (housing and social services) to break the cycle of violence.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Consistent tracking of shelter and housing placements ensures that donor funds are successfully transitioning survivors to safety.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 17.3% growth rate and expanding service areas, the foundation has significant capacity to absorb new funding for housing programs.

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates remarkable fiscal discipline, requiring only <cite id="6">$0.03 to raise each $1 in revenue</cite>, far outperforming industry averages.
TMWF holds a <cite id="10">perfect 100 score for Culture and Leadership</cite> and a <cite id="11">98/100 for Accountability</cite> from Charity Navigator.
In the 2024 reporting period, the foundation supported <cite id="12">137 emergency shelter residents</cite> and <cite id="13">216 non-residential domestic violence clients</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Texas Muslim Women's Foundation distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on domestic violence within the Muslim community, a historically underserved space. Their impact is quantifiable through consistent year-over-year reporting; for instance, in 2022 they provided <cite id="14">emergency shelter to 163 survivors and children</cite>, a figure that grew to <cite id="4">186 survivors in 2023</cite>. This growth is supported by a <cite id="15">3-year revenue CAGR of 17.3%</cite>, indicating a healthy expansion of their donor base and operational capacity. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="16">3.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient financial cushion for their 24/7 crisis operations.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2005, the Texas Muslim Women's Foundation (TMWF) has evolved into a critical social service provider in North Texas, specifically addressing the intersection of <cite id="1">domestic violence and community well-being</cite>. The organization operates with a high degree of financial efficiency, maintaining an <cite id="2">overall score of 98.98.66666666666667/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. TMWF provides a comprehensive safety net that includes <cite id="3">emergency shelters, transitional housing, and social services</cite> tailored to the cultural and linguistic needs of the Muslim community while remaining open to all. In 2023, the foundation demonstrated significant scale, providing <cite id="4">emergency shelter to 186 survivors</cite> and supporting <cite id="5">204 individuals through transitional or rapid re-housing programs</cite>. Their financial model is notably lean, spending only <cite id="6">$0.03 to raise every $1 in contributions</cite>, which allows for a high <cite id="7">program expense ratio of 86.0%</cite>. As a <cite id="8">zakat-eligible organization</cite>, TMWF serves as a vital bridge for Muslim donors seeking to fulfill religious obligations through domestic humanitarian work, specifically targeting <cite id="9">the poor (fuqara), the needy (masakin), and wayfarers (ibn sabil)</cite>.
